title: cyathea plicata
intro: Cyathea plicata, also known as the leather fern, is a species of fern in the family Cyatheaceae. This plant is a type of tree fern that is native to the tropical regions of Australia and New Caledonia, known for its unique and robust fronds. The Cyathea plicata has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, and it is often prepared as a tea or tincture for herbal use.
